title: Added --tls-verify functionality to podman search, with tests
body:
Functionality for search with tls verification:
if --tls-verify is false, HTTP is always allowed
if --tls-verify is true, HTTP is never allowed

if no --tls-verify is present, the following branch pattern is followed:
If --registry flag is present, and the registry specified is in registries.insecure in registries.conf, HTTP is allowed
If no registry flag, the insecure registries are compared with registries.search to find common elements. If any registries are shared, HTTP is allowed.

Fixes: #914 
Signed-off-by: haircommander <pehunt@redhat.com>

comments.body:
#929 and #924 are both my attempts at this. I messed each up, and this is the final PR ready for review. @TomSweeneyRedHat suggested we add creds and authfile params as well, but the scope of adding this functionality extends farther than this fix requires, so that can be a separate issue. I tried to match @mheon  's suggestion on how to handle tls-verify, and have updated the man page to reflect my decisions. In setting up tests for this, I also added functions to test/e2e/libpod_suite_test.go to stringify, arrayify and grep stderr because it was required to test whether this flag was working correctly (docker client library spits errors in stderr and the search doesn't fail with an error code). 

PTAL @rhatdan and all others tagged :smile: !

@haircommander could you update the git commit to reflect what you are changing and what the end behavior will look like.  I'm on PTO so I haven't reviewed the code but the way this should work is:

* If the user lists a fq registry name in registries.conf under 'registry' only, it is searchable and considered to be https only.
* If a user adds a fq registry name to the conf under 'insecure' only, the registry is considered to NOT be searchable and http only.
* If a user adds a fq registry name to both, it is considered to be both searchable and http only.

I'm hoping that the last bullet is the bug that was reported because it is probably a use case I missed when implementing this.
@baude I updated the original commit comment to reflect what I've done, and changed the functionality to better match how you describe the registries.conf works. PTAL when you're not vacationing :)

Approved by: baude
Pushing b43677c9fd7f04c1ebf8265a0b14fc8ed70e4d66 to master...
@haircommander I'd do work on a fresh branch/PR - I don't know how Homu would like re-merging a merged PR
(BTW should this work also update `LibpodAPI.SearchImage`?  (I have no idea, just asking. This does not mean ”$somebody needs to do that”.))
On `LibpodAPI`: good question. In an ideal world, the code is shared enough that we don't need to make changes in two places, but I suspect our current situation is rather far from ideal...
